title: UK Holiday Bookings Plummet Amid Cost of Living Crisis and Iran War Impact
detail: UK travel industry reports significant decline in holiday bookings as consumers cut discretionary spending due to persistent cost of living pressures. The ongoing Iran conflict has further dampened consumer confidence and increased travel insurance costs, creating a double impact on the leisure sector. This trend reflects broader economic stress as households prioritize essential spending over travel and entertainment.

media.caption: UK consumers cutting back on holiday spending amid economic pressures and geopolitical uncertainty
